summary: A guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed that HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\TaskbarAnimations = 0 disables taskbar animations and = 1 enables them on this build. Procmon then captured explorer.exe querying the same value with Data:0 and Data:1 in separate reversible passes, and a Ghidra headless pass on Taskbar.dll decompiled a code path that reads TaskbarAnimations from Explorer\Advanced. The app's current write matches the observed disabled state.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: taskbaranimations-state-0.txt: explorer.exe RegQueryValue ...\TaskbarAnimations Data: 0. taskbaranimations-state-1.txt: explorer.exe RegQueryValue ...\TaskbarAnimations Data: 1. taskbar-taskbaranimations-ghidra.md: Taskbar.dll reads Explorer\\Advanced\\TaskbarAnimations through Ordinal_123(..., L\"TaskbarAnimations\", 1).
validation_proof.notes: The reversible probe confirmed the 0 / 1 mapping, Procmon showed explorer.exe reading both states on Win25H2Clean, and a Ghidra pass on Taskbar.dll exposed a code-side read of TaskbarAnimations from Explorer\Advanced.

setting.name: Taskbar Animations
setting.casual_explanation: This controls whether Windows animates taskbar transitions. People turn it off when they want a snappier, less animated desktop.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable taskbar animations to reduce visual motion and make the shell feel a little faster.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling taskbar animations reduces visual motion, but it can make the shell feel less polished to users who prefer animations.
setting.professional_notes: The current app writes TaskbarAnimations = 0 under Explorer\Advanced. A reversible guest probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed that 0 disables the setting and 1 enables it. Procmon then captured explorer.exe querying the same value with Data:0 and Data:1 after Explorer restarts, which gives direct runtime proof that the shell consumes this flag on this build. A Ghidra headless pass on Taskbar.dll found the TaskbarAnimations string and decompiled a function that routes to Explorer\Advanced and reads TaskbarAnimations through Ordinal_123 after checking taskbar animation state through SystemParametersInfoW.

allowed_values.label: Taskbar animations off
allowed_values.meaning: Disables taskbar animations. The current app writes 0 here, the reversible guest probe confirmed that 0 is the off state, and Procmon captured explorer.exe reading Data:0 from the same value.

allowed_values.label: Taskbar animations on
allowed_values.meaning: Enables taskbar animations. The guest probe confirmed that 1 is the on state, and Procmon captured explorer.exe reading Data:1 from the same value.

targets.notes: TaskbarAnimations uses a binary 0 / 1 mapping on Win25H2Clean. This record documents the observed state transitions.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes TaskbarAnimations = 0, and the guest probe confirmed that 0 disables taskbar animations on Win25H2Clean.

windows_defaults.label: Windows baseline observed in guest

states.rationale: Guest baseline on Win25H2Clean was 0 and the reversible probe restored it to 0.

windows_defaults.notes: The observed baseline on Win25H2Clean was TaskbarAnimations=0.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable Taskbar Animations
intended_for: Users who want a less animated desktop
People tuning the shell for a snappier feel
avoid_for: Users who prefer the default animated shell

states.rationale: Set TaskbarAnimations=0 to disable taskbar animations.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want taskbar transitions to be less animated.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the default animated shell behavior.
tradeoffs: Reduces visual motion in the shell.

evidence.title: Guest reversible probe - TaskbarAnimations registry mapping
evidence.summary: Guest reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed TaskbarAnimations transitions through 0 and 1 before restoring to 0.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - explorer.exe TaskbarAnimations runtime reads
evidence.summary: Separate reversible Procmon captures on Win25H2Clean showed explorer.exe querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\TaskbarAnimations with Data:0 and Data:1 after Explorer restarts. The repo now keeps the filtered capture files directly under research/evidence-files/procmon/taskbar-animations-procmon-validation-20260326/.

evidence.title: Our Ghidra decompilation - Taskbar.dll TaskbarAnimations read path
evidence.summary: A headless Ghidra pass on Taskbar.dll found the TaskbarAnimations string and decompiled a function that reads SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\TaskbarAnimations through Ordinal_123 after checking taskbar animation state with SystemParametersInfoW. That gives direct code-side proof that the taskbar consumes this flag on this build.

evidence.title: Repo source note for performance.disable-taskbar-animations
evidence.summary: The repo source note links TaskbarAnimations to upstream nohuto research and marks the tweak as repo-backed.

decision.why: The guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed the 0 / 1 mapping for TaskbarAnimations, Procmon captured explorer.exe reading both states from the same value, Taskbar.dll decompiled to a direct TaskbarAnimations read under Explorer\Advanced, and the current app write matches the observed disabled state with a clean restore story.
